one_time_description: ISC Health Centre of Excellence capital contribution $30,385,015 and ISC COVID domes $8,306,603 recognized as capital contributions above the operating surplus line
economic_notes: Entity operates enterprises including pharmacy, inn, diner, fishing lodge, broadband internet, gaming commission, business development corporation, and real estate holdings. COVID-19 pandemic impacted operations particularly VLT revenues which dropped significantly from $24.3M to $11.1M.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (not 2024). Two large capital contributions recognized separately above the line: ISC Health Centre of Excellence $30,385,015 and ISC COVID domes $8,306,603, inflating surplus significantly. VLT revenue dropped from $24.3M to $11.1M due to COVID-19 closures. Entity changed amortization method in current year resulting in ~$4.5M increase in amortization expense. Complex consolidation includes ~20 subsidiaries (incorporated and unincorporated) and 2 partnerships (Bison Container Homes LLP and GP) accounted for under modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration was included in the extracted text. expenses_housing includes Housing $5,086,678 and CMHC $4,882,937. total_government_transfers includes ISC $104,492,338 + Province of Manitoba $2,293,416 + MKO $2,499,859 + CMHC $1,354,695 + Assembly of Manitoba Chiefs $1,755,713 + Manitoba Hydro $2,785,513 (partially government) = estimated; ISC COVID and HCE capital contributions included in total. OSR calculated as total revenue $150,896,860 minus estimated government transfers.
